title: Wholesome Korean Rice Bowl
description: A garlicky, pillowy Korean rice bowl with dried chillies and robata grilled tofu and tempeh, plant-based and festive.
instructions: Rinse 2 cups of short-grain rice under cold water until runoff is clear.
In a Dutch oven combine the rinsed rice with 4 cups of water, cover, and bring to a rolling boil over high heat.
While the rice begins to boil, heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a separate pan over medium-high heat.
Add the cubed tofu and sliced tempeh to the hot oil and sear each side until golden brown and slightly charred, then set aside.
In the same pan, add the minced garlic and crushed dried chillies and sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in 2 tablespoons of soy sauce and 1 tablespoon of sesame oil, stirring to combine the aromatics.
Reduce the heat to low, pour the soy sauce mixture over the boiling rice, stir well, and return to a simmer.
Add the mixed vegetables to the pot, cover, and cook for 10 minutes, allowing the rice to absorb the liquid and become pillowy.
Finally, fold the seared tofu and tempeh into the rice, reheating for 2 minutes so the protein warms through.
Serve hot, garnished with sliced scallions if desired.
